query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1912 (commit: 1985077b).

## Issue to Resolve

Add `Toggle auto advance` control/shortcut
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Sometimes during review you need to pause Auto Advance, unlike Anki desktop which I only click on shift+A to pause/resume the feature, on Ankidroid you need to exit the review session and toggle it, which is not practical at all.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A button to switch Auto Advance like the Enable/Disable whiteboard feature during review.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
x
**Additional context**
x

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

~~It should be under `Settings - Reviewing`~~
I'd like to work on it

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/reviewer/AutomaticAnswer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_controls.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
**Implementing auto advance toggle**

I need to implement a toggle for the auto advance feature in the reviewer. First, I‚Äôll inspect the code and read through the tools list. I won‚Äôt be using git commands for this. I plan to add a control button and a shortcut, likely using the viewer commands framework. The auto advance feature will automatically provide answers after a certain time, which I might label as "AutomaticAnswer." I‚Äôll also search for the current whiteboard toggle to guide me while inspecting the repository.

content.thinking:
**Inspecting preferences and commands**

I see that grep didn't find the preferences file names, possibly because of the output_mode files. I plan to check the relevant XML files. I need to update the controls settings‚Äîmaybe adding a shortcut like shift+A? But then, Android uses key combinations in the ViewerCommand mapping, so I need to identify the existing commands and their control mapping. Time to inspect ViewerCommand.kt for more details!

 37‚Üíenum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 38‚Üí    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),

 87‚Üí    USER_ACTION_9(R.string.user_action_9)
 88‚Üí    ;
 89‚Üí
 90‚Üí    companion object {
 91‚Üí        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 92‚Üí            get() = Arrays.stream(entries.toTypedArray())
 93‚Üí                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 94‚Üí                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí        fun fromPreferenceKey(key: String) = entries.first { it.preferenceKey == key }
 97‚Üí    }
 98‚Üí
 99‚Üí    val preferenceKey: String
100‚Üí        get() = "binding_$name"
101‚Üí
102‚Üí    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
103‚Üí        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
104‚Üí            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
105‚Üí            collection.remove(element)
106‚Üí            collection.add(0, element)
107‚Üí            true
108‚Üí        }
109‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
110‚Üí    }
111‚Üí
112‚Üí    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
113‚Üí        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
114‚Üí            // do not reorder the elements
115‚Üí            if (collection.contains(element)) {
116‚Üí                return@BiFunction false
117‚Üí            }
118‚Üí            collection.add(element)
119‚Üí            return@BiFunction true
120‚Üí        }
121‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
122‚Üí    }
123‚Üí
124‚Üí    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
125‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
126‚Üí        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
127‚Üí        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
128‚Üí        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
129‚Üí    }
130‚Üí
131‚Üí    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
132‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
133‚Üí        bindings.remove(binding)
134‚Üí        prefs.edit {
135‚Üí            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
136‚Üí        }
137‚Üí    }
138‚Üí
139‚Üí    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
140‚Üí    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
141‚Üí        get() {
142‚Üí            // all of the default commands are currently for the Reviewer
143‚Üí            fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, modifierKeys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()) =
144‚Üí                keyCode(keycode, Screen.Reviewer(side), modifierKeys)
145‚Üí            fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide) = unicode(c, Screen.Reviewer(side))
146‚Üí            return when (this) {
147‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> listOf(
148‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
149‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
150‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
151‚Üí                )
152‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> listOf(
153‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
154‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
155‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
156‚Üí                )
157‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> listOf(
158‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
159‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
160‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
161‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
162‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
163‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
164‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
165‚Üí                )
166‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> listOf(
167‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
168‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
169‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
170‚Üí                )
171‚Üí                EDIT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
172‚Üí                MARK -> listOf(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
173‚Üí                BURY_CARD -> listOf(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
174‚Üí                BURY_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
175‚Üí                SUSPEND_CARD -> listOf(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
176‚Üí                SUSPEND_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
177‚Üí                PLAY_MEDIA -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
178‚Üí                REPLAY_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
179‚Üí                RECORD_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
180‚Üí                SAVE_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_S, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
181‚Üí                UNDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
182‚Üí                REDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH, ModifierKeys(shift = true, ctrl = true, alt = false)))
183‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
184‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
185‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
186‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
187‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
188‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
189‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
190‚Üí                SHOW_HINT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
191‚Üí                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
192‚Üí                ADD_NOTE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
193‚Üí                else -> emptyList()
194‚Üí            }
195‚Üí        }
196‚Üí
197‚Üí    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, screen: Screen, keys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()): MappableBinding {
198‚Üí        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), screen)
199‚Üí    }
200‚Üí
201‚Üí    private fun unicode(c: Char, screen: Screen): MappableBinding {
202‚Üí        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), screen)
203‚Üí    }
204‚Üí
205‚Üí    fun interface CommandProcessor {
206‚Üí        /**
207‚Üí         * @return whether the command was executed
208‚Üí         */
209‚Üí        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
210‚Üí    }
211‚Üí}

 38‚Üí/**
 39‚Üí * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 40‚Üí * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 41‚Üí *
 42‚Üí * ## Settings
 43‚Üí * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 44‚Üí *
 45‚Üí * ## Implementation
 46‚Üí *
 47‚Üí * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 48‚Üí * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 49‚Üí * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 50‚Üí *
 51‚Üí * When a card is displayed (Front)
 52‚Üí *
 53‚Üí * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 54‚Üí * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 55‚Üí *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 56‚Üí * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 57‚Üí *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 58‚Üí *
 59‚Üí * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 60‚Üí *
 61‚Üí * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 62‚Üí *
 63‚Üí * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 64‚Üí * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 65‚Üí *
 66‚Üí * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 67‚Üí * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 68‚Üí */
 69‚Üíclass AutomaticAnswer(
 70‚Üí    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 71‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 72‚Üí) {
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üí    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 75‚Üí     *
 76‚Üí     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 77‚Üí     */
 78‚Üí    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 79‚Üí        private set
 80‚Üí
 81‚Üí    /**
 82‚Üí     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 83‚Üí     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 84‚Üí     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 85‚Üí     */
 86‚Üí    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 87‚Üí
 88‚Üí    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 89‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 90‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 91‚Üí            return@Runnable
 92‚Üí        }
 93‚Üí        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 96‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 97‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 98‚Üí            return@Runnable
 99‚Üí        }
100‚Üí        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    /**
104‚Üí     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
105‚Üí     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
106‚Üí     */
107‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
108‚Üí    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
109‚Üí
110‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
111‚Üí    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
112‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
113‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
114‚Üí            return
115‚Üí        }
116‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
117‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
118‚Üí    }
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
121‚Üí    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
122‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
123‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
124‚Üí            return
125‚Üí        }
126‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
127‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
128‚Üí    }
129‚Üí
130‚Üí    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
131‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
132‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
133‚Üí    }
134‚Üí
135‚Üí    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
136‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
137‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
138‚Üí    }
139‚Üí
140‚Üí    fun enable() {
141‚Üí        isDisabled = false
142‚Üí    }
143‚Üí
144‚Üí    fun disable() {
145‚Üí        isDisabled = true
146‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
147‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
148‚Üí    }
149‚Üí
150‚Üí    /** Stop any "Automatic show answer" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
151‚Üí    fun onDisplayQuestion() {
152‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
153‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion) return
154‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = false
155‚Üí
156‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
157‚Üí    }
158‚Üí
159‚Üí    /** Stop any "Automatic show question" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
160‚Üí    fun onDisplayAnswer() {
161‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
162‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer) return
163‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = false
164‚Üí
165‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
166‚Üí    }
167‚Üí
168‚Üí    // region TODO: These attempt to stop a race condition between a manual answer and the automated answer
169‚Üí    // I don't believe this is thread-safe
170‚Üí
171‚Üí    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has selected an answer */
172‚Üí    fun onSelectEase() {
173‚Üí        Timber.v("onSelectEase")
174‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
175‚Üí    }
176‚Üí
177‚Üí    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has flipped the card */
178‚Üí    fun onShowAnswer() {
179‚Üí        Timber.v("onShowAnswer")
180‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
181‚Üí    }
182‚Üí
183‚Üí    // endregion
184‚Üí
185‚Üí    /**
186‚Üí     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowAnswer]
187‚Üí     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
188‚Üí     */
189‚Üí    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer(additionalDelay: Long = 0) {
190‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
191‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion) return
192‚Üí        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
193‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = true
194‚Üí        delayedShowAnswer(settings.millisecondsToShowQuestionFor + additionalDelay)
195‚Üí    }
196‚Üí
197‚Üí    /**
198‚Üí     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowQuestion]
199‚Üí     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
200‚Üí     */
201‚Üí    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion(additionalMediaDelay: Long = 0) {
202‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
203‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer) return
204‚Üí        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
205‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = true
206‚Üí        delayedShowQuestion(settings.millisecondsToShowAnswerFor + additionalMediaDelay)
207‚Üí    }
208‚Üí
209‚Üí    fun isEnabled(): Boolean {
210‚Üí        return settings.useTimer
211‚Üí    }
212‚Üí
213‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
214‚Üí    internal fun simulateCardFlip() {
215‚Üí        Timber.d("simulateCardFlip")
216‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = false
217‚Üí    }
218‚Üí
219‚Üí    interface AutomaticallyAnswered {
220‚Üí        fun automaticShowAnswer()
221‚Üí        fun automaticShowQuestion(action: AutomaticAnswerAction)
222‚Üí    }
223‚Üí
224‚Üí    companion object {
225‚Üí        @CheckResult
226‚Üí        fun defaultInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered): AutomaticAnswer {
227‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswer(target, AutomaticAnswerSettings())
228‚Üí        }
229‚Üí
230‚Üí        @CheckResult
231‚Üí        fun createInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered, preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswer {
232‚Üí            val settings = AutomaticAnswerSettings.createInstance(preferences, col)
233‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswer(target, settings)
234‚Üí        }
235‚Üí    }
236‚Üí}
237‚Üí
238‚Üí/**
239‚Üí * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
240‚Üí *
241‚Üí * * Enabled
242‚Üí * * Time to show answer (20s)
243‚Üí * * Time to show next question (60s)
244‚Üí * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
245‚Üí *
246‚Üí * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
247‚Üí *
248‚Üí * These settings also exist in an options group
249‚Üí * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
250‚Üí *
251‚Üí * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
252‚Üí * * Enabled, etc...
253‚Üí */
254‚Üíclass AutomaticAnswerSettings(
255‚Üí    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
256‚Üí    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
257‚Üí    private val secondsToShowQuestionFor: Double = 60.0,
258‚Üí    private val secondsToShowAnswerFor: Double = 20.0
259‚Üí) {
260‚Üí
261‚Üí    val millisecondsToShowQuestionFor = (secondsToShowQuestionFor * 1000L).toLong()
262‚Üí    val millisecondsToShowAnswerFor = (secondsToShowAnswerFor * 1000L).toLong()
263‚Üí
264‚Üí    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
265‚Üí    val autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer; get() = secondsToShowAnswerFor > 0
266‚Üí    val autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion; get() = secondsToShowQuestionFor > 0
267‚Üí
268‚Üí    companion object {
269‚Üí        /**
270‚Üí         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config
271‚Üí         */
272‚Üí        @NeedsTest("ensure question setting maps to question parameter")
273‚Üí        fun queryOptions(
274‚Üí            preferences: SharedPreferences,
275‚Üí            col: Collection,
276‚Üí            selectedDid: DeckId
277‚Üí        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
278‚Üí            val conf = col.decks.configDictForDeckId(selectedDid)
279‚Üí            val action = getAction(conf)
280‚Üí            val useTimer = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
281‚Üí
282‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(
283‚Üí                answerAction = action,
284‚Üí                useTimer = useTimer,
285‚Üí                secondsToShowQuestionFor = conf.secondsToShowQuestion,
286‚Üí                secondsToShowAnswerFor = conf.secondsToShowAnswer
287‚Üí            )
288‚Üí        }
289‚Üí
290‚Üí        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
291‚Üí            return queryOptions(preferences, col, col.decks.selected())
292‚Üí        }
293‚Üí
294‚Üí        private fun getAction(conf: DeckConfig): AutomaticAnswerAction {
295‚Üí            return try {
296‚Üí                val value: Int = conf.optInt(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY)
297‚Üí                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromConfigValue(value)
298‚Üí            } catch (e: Exception) {
299‚Üí                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
300‚Üí            }
301‚Üí        }
302‚Üí    }
303‚Üí}
304‚Üí
305‚Üí/**
306‚Üí * Represents a value from [anki.deck_config.DeckConfig.Config.AnswerAction]
307‚Üí * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
308‚Üí */
309‚Üíenum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val configValue: Int) {
310‚Üí    /** Default: least invasive action */
311‚Üí    BURY_CARD(0),
312‚Üí    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
313‚Üí    ANSWER_GOOD(2),
314‚Üí    ANSWER_HARD(3),
315‚Üí    SHOW_REMINDER(4);
316‚Üí
317‚Üí    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
318‚Üí        val action = this.toCommand()
319‚Üí        if (action != null) {
320‚Üí            Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
321‚Üí            reviewer.executeCommand(action)
322‚Üí        } else {
323‚Üí            reviewer.showSnackbar(TR.studyingAnswerTimeElapsed())
324‚Üí        }
325‚Üí    }
326‚Üí
327‚Üí    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
328‚Üí    private fun toCommand(): ViewerCommand? {
329‚Üí        return when (this) {
330‚Üí            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
331‚Üí            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand()
332‚Üí            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand()
333‚Üí            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand()
334‚Üí            else -> null
335‚Üí        }
336‚Üí    }
337‚Üí
338‚Üí    companion object {
339‚Üí        /**

343‚Üí         */
344‚Üí        const val CONFIG_KEY = "answerAction"
345‚Üí
346‚Üí        /** convert from [anki.deck_config.DeckConfig.Config.AnswerAction] to the enum */
347‚Üí        fun fromConfigValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
348‚Üí            return entries.firstOrNull { it.configValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD

353‚Üí                onEditedNoteChanged()
354‚Üí            } else if (result.resultCode == RESULT_CANCELED && !reloadRequired) {

363‚Üí    ) : ActivityResultCallback<ActivityResult> {
364‚Üí        override fun onActivityResult(result: ActivityResult) {
365‚Üí            if (result.resultCode == DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR) {
366‚Üí                closeReviewer(DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR)
367‚Üí            }
368‚Üí            if (result.resultCode == DeckPicker.RESULT_MEDIA_EJECTED) {
369‚Üí                finishNoStorageAvailable()
370‚Üí            }
371‚Üí
372‚Üí            /* Reset the schedule and reload the latest card off the top of the stack if required.

374‚Üí               note type could have lead to the card being deleted */
375‚Üí            val reloadRequired =
376‚Üí                result.data?.getBooleanExtra(NoteEditor.RELOAD_REQUIRED_EXTRA_KEY, false) == true
377‚Üí            if (reloadRequired) {
378‚Üí                performReload()
379‚Üí            }
380‚Üí
381‚Üí            callback(result, reloadRequired)
382‚Üí        }
383‚Üí    }

396‚Üí            if (event.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_DOWN) {
397‚Üí                // Save states when button pressed

399‚Üí                hasBeenTouched = true
400‚Üí                // We will need to check if a touch is followed by a click
401‚Üí                // Since onTouch always come before onClick, we should check if
402‚Üí                // the touch is going to be a click by storing the start coordinates

404‚Üí                touchX = event.rawX
405‚Üí                touchY = event.rawY
406‚Üí            } else if (event.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
407‚Üí                val diffX = abs(event.rawX - touchX)
408‚Üí                val diffY = abs(event.rawY - touchY)
409‚Üí                // If a click is not coming then we reset the touch
410‚Üí                if (diffX > Companion.CLICK_ACTION_THRESHOLD || diffY > Companion.CLICK_ACTION_THRESHOLD) {
411‚Üí                    hasBeenTouched = false
412‚Üí                }
413‚Üí            }
414‚Üí            return false
415‚Üí        }

content.thinking:
**Evaluating auto advance options**

I'm thinking about the settings for automatic answers and what toggling auto advance means. It seems like it temporarily disables or enables scheduling without changing preferences. For Anki's desktop, Shift+A is meant to toggle auto advance, which appears to be based on deck options. To help reviewers, I might want to add a command to allow toggling of auto advance. We could implement a new viewer command, "TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE," while making sure there's no key conflict with existing functions.**Implementing command processing**

I'm thinking about how to implement the command processor in the AbstractFlashcardViewer, specifically through the executeCommand function. I should search for where executeCommand is defined and switch to the proper handling of commands. It seems like focusing on this part of the code will help in making the necessary adjustments. I'll need to make sure everything integrates smoothly to allow for efficient command execution in the review process.

**Evaluating UI features**

I‚Äôm thinking about making sure that certain features are always available, but if they're not enabled, we could show a snack notification instead. It‚Äôs also important to include customizable buttons during reviews, possibly in the toolbar or at the bottom. I see there's an `ActionButtons` class in the reviewer package, but is it for the top bar? I need to check the ActionButtons and the preferences_controls.xml to locate the control settings.

22‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.R
23‚Üí
24‚Üíclass ActionButtons {
25‚Üí    // DEFECT: This should be private - it breaks the law of demeter, but it'll be a large refactoring to get
26‚Üí    // to this point
27‚Üí    val status: ActionButtonStatus = ActionButtonStatus()
28‚Üí    private var menu: Menu? = null
29‚Üí    fun setup(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
30‚Üí        status.setup(preferences)
31‚Üí    }
32‚Üí
33‚Üí    /** Sets the order of the Action Buttons in the action bar  */
34‚Üí    fun setCustomButtonsStatus(menu: Menu) {
35‚Üí        status.setCustomButtons(menu)
36‚Üí        this.menu = menu
37‚Üí    }
38‚Üí
39‚Üí    fun findMenuItem(@IdRes resId: Int) = menu?.findItem(resId) as? MenuItemImpl
40‚Üí
41‚Üí    companion object {
42‚Üí        @IdRes
43‚Üí        val RES_FLAG = R.id.action_flag
44‚Üí
45‚Üí        @IdRes
46‚Üí        val RES_MARK = R.id.action_mark_card
47‚Üí    }
48‚Üí}

320‚Üí            internal fun upgradeBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, binding: Binding) {
321‚Üí                Timber.d("Replacing gesture '%s' with binding", oldGesturePreferenceKey)
322‚Üí
323‚Üí                // This exists as a user may have mapped "volume down" to "UNDO".
324‚Üí                // Undo already exists as a key binding, and we don't want to trash this during an upgrade
325‚Üí                if (!preferences.contains(oldGesturePreferenceKey)) {
326‚Üí                    Timber.v("No preference to upgrade")
327‚Üí                    return
328‚Üí                }
329‚Üí
330‚Üí                try {
331‚Üí                    replaceBinding(preferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey, binding)
332‚Üí                } finally {
333‚Üí                    Timber.v("removing pref key: '%s'", oldGesturePreferenceKey)
334‚Üí                    // remove the old key
335‚Üí                    preferences.edit { remove(oldGesturePreferenceKey) }
336‚Üí                }
337‚Üí            }
338‚Üí
339‚Üí            private fun replaceBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, binding: Binding) {
340‚Üí                // the preference should be set, but if it's null, then we have nothing to do
341‚Üí                val pref = preferences.getString(oldGesturePreferenceKey, "0") ?: return
342‚Üí                // If the preference doesn't map (for example: it was removed), then nothing to do
343‚Üí                val asInt = pref.toIntOrNull() ?: return
344‚Üí                val command = oldCommandValues[asInt] ?: return
345‚Üí
346‚Üí                Timber.i("Moving preference from '%s' to '%s'", oldGesturePreferenceKey, command.preferenceKey)
347‚Üí
348‚Üí                // add to the binding_COMMANDNAME preference
349‚Üí                val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, command.screenBuilder(CardSide.BOTH))
350‚Üí                command.addBindingAtEnd(preferences, mappableBinding)
351‚Üí            }
352‚Üí        }

16‚Üípackage com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.autoadvance
17‚Üí
18‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
19‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.asyncIO
20‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.launchCatchingIO
21‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.ReviewerViewModel
22‚Üíimport com.ichi2.libanki.Card
23‚Üíimport kotlinx.coroutines.Job
24‚Üíimport kotlinx.coroutines.delay
25‚Üí
26‚Üí/**
27‚Üí * Implementation of the `Auto Advance` deck options
28‚Üí *
29‚Üí * A timer (in seconds) can be set to automatically trigger an action after it runs out,
30‚Üí * either in the question side ([QuestionAction]) or in the answer side ([AnswerAction]).
31‚Üí *
32‚Üí * If a timer is set to 0, the corresponding action is not triggered.
33‚Üí *
34‚Üí * @see AutoAdvanceSettings
35‚Üí */
36‚Üíclass AutoAdvance(val viewModel: ReviewerViewModel) {
37‚Üí    private var questionActionJob: Job? = null
38‚Üí    private var answerActionJob: Job? = null
39‚Üí
40‚Üí    private var settings = viewModel.asyncIO {
41‚Üí        val card = viewModel.currentCard.await()
42‚Üí        AutoAdvanceSettings.createInstance(card.currentDeckId().did)
43‚Üí    }
44‚Üí
45‚Üí    private suspend fun durationToShowQuestionFor() = settings.await().durationToShowQuestionFor
46‚Üí    private suspend fun durationToShowAnswerFor() = settings.await().durationToShowAnswerFor
47‚Üí    private suspend fun questionAction() = settings.await().questionAction
48‚Üí    private suspend fun answerAction() = settings.await().answerAction
49‚Üí    suspend fun shouldWaitForAudio() = settings.await().waitForAudio
50‚Üí
51‚Üí    fun cancelQuestionAndAnswerActionJobs() {
52‚Üí        questionActionJob?.cancel()
53‚Üí        answerActionJob?.cancel()
54‚Üí    }
55‚Üí
56‚Üí    fun onCardChange(card: Card) {
57‚Üí        cancelQuestionAndAnswerActionJobs()
58‚Üí        settings = viewModel.asyncIO {
59‚Üí            AutoAdvanceSettings.createInstance(card.currentDeckId().did)
60‚Üí        }
61‚Üí    }
62‚Üí
63‚Üí    suspend fun onShowQuestion() {
64‚Üí        answerActionJob?.cancel()
65‚Üí        if (!durationToShowQuestionFor().isPositive()) return
66‚Üí
67‚Üí        questionActionJob = viewModel.launchCatchingIO {
68‚Üí            delay(durationToShowQuestionFor())
69‚Üí            when (questionAction()) {
70‚Üí                QuestionAction.SHOW_ANSWER -> viewModel.showAnswer()
71‚Üí                QuestionAction.SHOW_REMINDER -> showReminder(TR.studyingQuestionTimeElapsed())
72‚Üí            }
73‚Üí        }
74‚Üí    }
75‚Üí
76‚Üí    suspend fun onShowAnswer() {
77‚Üí        questionActionJob?.cancel()
78‚Üí        if (!durationToShowAnswerFor().isPositive()) return
79‚Üí
80‚Üí        answerActionJob = viewModel.launchCatchingIO {
81‚Üí            delay(durationToShowAnswerFor())
82‚Üí            when (answerAction()) {
83‚Üí                AnswerAction.BURY_CARD -> viewModel.buryCard()
84‚Üí                AnswerAction.ANSWER_AGAIN -> viewModel.answerAgain()
85‚Üí                AnswerAction.ANSWER_HARD -> viewModel.answerHard()
86‚Üí                AnswerAction.ANSWER_GOOD -> viewModel.answerGood()
87‚Üí                AnswerAction.SHOW_REMINDER -> showReminder(TR.studyingAnswerTimeElapsed())
88‚Üí            }
89‚Üí        }
90‚Üí    }
91‚Üí
92‚Üí    private fun showReminder(message: String) {
93‚Üí        viewModel.launchCatchingIO {
94‚Üí            viewModel.actionFeedbackFlow.emit(message)
95‚Üí        }
96‚Üí    }
97‚Üí}
98‚Üí

content.thinking:
**Clarifying auto advance features**

I‚Äôm sorting through how auto advance is handled in the old and new reviewers. In the old reviewer, it seems that auto advance is still tied to an AutomaticAnswer system, while the new reviewer has introduced a separate AutoAdvance feature. I'm also checking the preferences and settings related to displaying answers automatically. It appears that both reviewers might use deck options, and these settings could be impacting how auto advance behaves. I‚Äôll need to search more for configuration details to clarify this.**Planning auto advance implementation**

I‚Äôm realizing that the deck option "Automatic Display Answer" might actually match the old deck option for auto advance. There‚Äôs a toggle for auto advance with a Shift+A command in Anki desktop, so I plan to implement a toggle for automatic answers in the viewer with question and answer timers. I‚Äôll create a new command for toggling auto advance and address whether to store its state temporarily or persist it across sessions. It's a good idea to save it in the state during review but maybe not beyond that.**Implementing scheduling logic**

I'm planning to use the methods for toggling the auto advance feature, but I need to be careful with the disable() call since it cancels tasks. On re-enabling, it sets isDisabled to false but doesn't reschedule, so I should trigger scheduling based on the current card display. I‚Äôll call displayCardQuestion/Answer to manage this. When enabling, I‚Äôll also ensure to schedule tasks appropriately. In the old viewer, I'll look into when and where scheduling happens, particularly with the methods related to automatic answers.

 70‚Üí    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 71‚Üí        customButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(

 74‚Üí        )!!.toInt()
 75‚Üí    }

 77‚Üí    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 78‚Üí        for ((itemId, value) in customButtons) {

 85‚Üí        }

content.thinking:
**Considering menu item setup**

Alright, I need to think about adding a new menu item related to ActionButtonStatus setup. It seems like a custom button configuration could be consistent, but keeping showAsAction minimal is important. ActionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus adjusts status based on entries, but if it‚Äôs not possible to customize, that‚Äôs okay. The reviewer menu requires default showAsAction set to never, showing options in overflow. I‚Äôm contemplating how things like the whiteboard toggle work with action configurations, which adds another layer to this decision.**Inspecting reviewer menu resources**

I need to look into adding an auto advance feature that‚Äôs similar to something we‚Äôve done before. It seems there‚Äôs a new reviewer fragment menu in layout res/menu/reviewer2 that I should check out. I have to inspect the new reviewer menu resources to understand how it‚Äôs structured. I'll search specifically in the res/menu directory to see what‚Äôs there and how it might fit into my plans. This should help clarify things!

I need to add a menu item for toggling auto advance in `ReviewerFragment.onMenuItemClick`. My plan is to implement `viewModel.toggleAutoAdvance()`, possibly using state flow for the title, although dynamic title changes might not be crucial. The button should say "Toggle auto advance," without dynamic titles. Implementing command in the old reviewer might not need a menu, but since a button was requested, I‚Äôll go ahead and add one. I'll also need to enable shortcut support via a key binding system.**Locating command key strings**

I need to find strings like `show_answer_command_key`. I'll start by looking in the `res/values/` directory, probably in the `10-preferences.xml` file. My goal is to search specifically for occurrences of `_command_key`. This search should help me identify the various command key strings we have in the resource files, ensuring I gather all the necessary components for whatever implementation or changes I need to make!

content.thinking:
**Evaluating audio scheduling**

I realize that the waitForAudio option means I should wait for the audio to finish before starting the timer. Scheduling while audio is playing could cut it short, which isn't great. I think it's better to schedule only when audio isn't playing, but I can't quite detect that. Minimal adjustment would be to treat unpausing as if waitForAudio is false. In Anki desktop, auto advance just stops the countdown but resumes from full, which is okay. So, I'll implement unpausing with the appropriate task scheduling.**Clarifying access to functions**

I see that AbstractFlashcardViewer is in one package and AutomaticAnswer in another, but since they're in the same module, functions can be accessible across packages. The `internal fun simulateCardFlip()` should be callable, but it‚Äôs marked for testing, so it might not be intended for regular use. 

I plan to add a public `fun resetSoundPlaybackState()` for clarity. Then I‚Äôll modify AutomaticAnswer with `fun setUserPaused(paused: Boolean)` to manage the paused state, ensuring we also store it for reviewer instances. I‚Äôll also look into restoring the paused state after a potential rotation.**Creating a shortcut for auto-advance**

I need to create a shortcut by adding to the default mapping in ViewerCommand. I'm thinking of using shift+A for both the question and answer sides, which should work fine. I'll set the keyCode to KEYCODE_A and specify CardSide.BOTH. In the `TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE`, I'll include this in the switch statement.

Next, I should update `preferences_controls.xml` for the mapping and possibly tweak `BindingPreferences`. I also want to add a menu item in the old reviewer with the ID action_toggle_auto_advance, maybe placing it near other options. I'll use a title like @string/pause_auto_advance and consider an icon, though it's not strictly necessary. Implementing this in Reviewer.onCreateOptionsMenu will help ensure the title reflects the user's paused state.**Implementing auto advance visibility**

I need to keep in mind that enabling auto advance relies on `automaticAnswer.isEnabled()`, which checks the timer settings from preferences and deck config. It‚Äôs possible to have the timer enabled but set to zero, meaning auto advance wouldn‚Äôt actually occur even if visible. To avoid clutter, I‚Äôll make the visibility dependent on whether `automaticAnswer.isEnabled()`. 

Next, I‚Äôll update Reviewer.onOptionsItemSelected to handle the action_toggle_auto_advance by calling `executeCommand(ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE)`, which will trigger the `toggleAutoAdvance()` method. I'll implement this method in AbstractFlashcardViewer to create the toggle functionality, ensuring it refreshes the action bar correctly when needed.**Finalizing toggle auto advance logic**

In the `toggleAutoAdvance()` method, I plan to implement several checks. First, if `automaticAnswer.isEnabled()` returns false, I might show a snackbar to indicate that auto advance isn‚Äôt enabled. If `isUserPaused` is true, I would resume and notify the user; otherwise, I‚Äôd pause the auto advance and update them accordingly.

After toggling, I need to reset the scheduling properly based on whether audio should wait. I‚Äôll implement logic to handle scheduling right after resuming, ensuring it accounts for the current display state. In the ReviewerFragment/ViewModel, I‚Äôll create a way to manage user pauses with a MutableStateFlow and provide feedback during toggling.

 71‚Üí    @StringRes textResource: Int,
 72‚Üí    duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_LONG,

 79‚Üí/**
 80‚Üí * Show a snackbar.
 81‚Üí *
 82‚Üí * You can create snackbars by calling `showSnackbar` on either an activity or a view.
 83‚Üí * As `CoordinatorLayout` is responsible for proper placement and animation of snackbars,
 84‚Üí *
 85‚Üí *   * if calling on an activity, the activity **MUST** have a `CoordinatorLayout`
 86‚Üí *     with id `root_layout`;
 87‚Üí *
 88‚Üí *   * if calling on a view, the view **MUST** be either a `CoordinatorLayout`,
 89‚Üí *     or a (possibly indirect) child of `CoordinatorLayout`.
 90‚Üí *
 91‚Üí * Any additional configuration can be done in the configuration block, e.g.
 92‚Üí *

100‚Üí *     [Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT], [Snackbar.LENGTH_LONG] (default), [Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE],
101‚Üí *     or exact duration in milliseconds.
102‚Üí * @param snackbarBuilder Optional. A configuration block with the [Snackbar] as `this`.
103‚Üí */
104‚Üífun Activity.showSnackbar(
105‚Üí    text: CharSequence,
106‚Üí    duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_LONG,
107‚Üí    snackbarBuilder: SnackbarBuilder? = null
108‚Üí) {
109‚Üí    val view: View? = findViewById(R.id.root_layout) as? CoordinatorLayout
110‚Üí
111‚Üí    if (view != null) {
112‚Üí        val baseSnackbarBuilder = (this as? BaseSnackbarBuilderProvider)?.baseSnackbarBuilder
113‚Üí        view.showSnackbar(text, duration) {
114‚Üí            baseSnackbarBuilder?.invoke(this)
115‚Üí            snackbarBuilder?.invoke(this)
116‚Üí            Timber.d("displayed snackbar: '%s'", text)
117‚Üí        }
118‚Üí    } else {
119‚Üí        val errorMessage = "While trying to show a snackbar, " +
120‚Üí            "could not find a view with id root_layout in $this"
121‚Üí
122‚Üí        if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
123‚Üí            throw IllegalArgumentException(errorMessage)
124‚Üí        } else {
125‚Üí            Timber.e(errorMessage)
126‚Üí            showThemedToast(this, text, false)
127‚Üí        }
128‚Üí    }

115‚Üí            // do not reorder the elements

content.text:
Implemented an in-review **Toggle Auto Advance** control + shortcut.

### Key behavior
- Adds a new `ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE` with default shortcut **Shift + A**.
- While reviewing, toggling **pauses/resumes** auto-advance timers without leaving the session.
- Works in both the legacy `Reviewer` and the newer `ReviewerFragment` reviewer.

### Main changes
- **Auto-advance pause state**
  - `AutomaticAnswer` now supports user pause/resume (`isPausedByUser`, `pauseByUser()`, `resumeByUser()`), and scheduling respects this without ‚Äúconsuming‚Äù the timer state.
- **Command + shortcut**
  - `ViewerCommand` includes `TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE` with default binding **Shift+A**.
- **UI buttons**
  - Added `action_toggle_auto_advance` to both `res/menu/reviewer.xml` and `res/menu/reviewer2.xml`.
  - Legacy reviewer updates the menu title to ‚ÄúPause/Resume auto advance‚Äù based on current state.
- **Controls settings**
  - Added binding key + control entry in `preferences.xml` and `preferences_controls.xml` so users can remap it.
- **Analytics**
  - Added `binding_TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE` to `UsageAnalytics`.

### Files touched (high level)
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/reviewer/AutomaticAnswer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ui/windows/reviewer/autoadvance/AutoAdvance.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ui/windows/reviewer/ReviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ui/windows/reviewer/ReviewerFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/menu/reviewer.xml`, `reviewer2.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml`, `11-arrays.xml`, `preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_controls.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`

 38‚Üí/**
 39‚Üí * AnkiDroid contains a setting: "Automatic display answer" which displays the
 40‚Üí * question/answer automatically after a set period of time.
 41‚Üí *
 42‚Üí * ## Settings
 43‚Üí * See: [AutomaticAnswerSettings]
 44‚Üí *
 45‚Üí * ## Implementation
 46‚Üí *
 47‚Üí * If "Enabled" is disabled, then nothing happens
 48‚Üí * If Time to show Answer is 0, the automatic flipping code is disabled
 49‚Üí * If Time to show next question is 0, the automatic answering code is disabled
 50‚Üí *
 51‚Üí * When a card is displayed (Front)
 52‚Üí *
 53‚Üí * If it has neither TTS nor audio, schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 54‚Üí * If it has audio, calculate the time for the audio to play
 55‚Üí *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" + "audio runtime" seconds
 56‚Üí * If it has TTS, add a handler to the completion of TTS
 57‚Üí *   * schedule the answer to display in "Time to show answer" seconds
 58‚Üí *
 59‚Üí * Do the same for the Back, but use the "Time to show next question" setting.
 60‚Üí *
 61‚Üí * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 62‚Üí *
 63‚Üí * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 64‚Üí * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 65‚Üí *
 66‚Üí * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 67‚Üí * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 68‚Üí */
 69‚Üíclass AutomaticAnswer(
 70‚Üí    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 71‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 72‚Üí) {
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üí    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 75‚Üí     *
 76‚Üí     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 77‚Üí     */
 78‚Üí    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 79‚Üí        private set
 80‚Üí
 81‚Üí    /**
 82‚Üí     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 83‚Üí     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 84‚Üí     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 85‚Üí     */
 86‚Üí    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 87‚Üí
 88‚Üí    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 89‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 90‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 91‚Üí            return@Runnable
 92‚Üí        }
 93‚Üí        target.automaticShowAnswer()
 94‚Üí    }
 95‚Üí    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
 96‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
 97‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
 98‚Üí            return@Runnable
 99‚Üí        }
100‚Üí        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
101‚Üí    }
102‚Üí
103‚Üí    /**
104‚Üí     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
105‚Üí     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
106‚Üí     */
107‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
108‚Üí    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
109‚Üí
110‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
111‚Üí    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
112‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
113‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
114‚Üí            return
115‚Üí        }
116‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
117‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
118‚Üí    }
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
121‚Üí    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
122‚Üí        if (isDisabled) {
123‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
124‚Üí            return
125‚Üí        }
126‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
127‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
128‚Üí    }
129‚Üí
130‚Üí    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
131‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
132‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
133‚Üí    }
134‚Üí
135‚Üí    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
136‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
137‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
138‚Üí    }
139‚Üí
140‚Üí    fun enable() {
141‚Üí        isDisabled = false
142‚Üí    }
143‚Üí
144‚Üí    fun disable() {
145‚Üí        isDisabled = true
146‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
147‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
148‚Üí    }
149‚Üí
150‚Üí    /** Stop any "Automatic show answer" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
151‚Üí    fun onDisplayQuestion() {
152‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
153‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion) return
154‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = false
155‚Üí
156‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
157‚Üí    }
158‚Üí
159‚Üí    /** Stop any "Automatic show question" tasks in order to avoid race conditions */
160‚Üí    fun onDisplayAnswer() {
161‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
162‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer) return
163‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = false
164‚Üí
165‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
166‚Üí    }
167‚Üí
168‚Üí    // region TODO: These attempt to stop a race condition between a manual answer and the automated answer
169‚Üí    // I don't believe this is thread-safe
170‚Üí
171‚Üí    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has selected an answer */
172‚Üí    fun onSelectEase() {
173‚Üí        Timber.v("onSelectEase")
174‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
175‚Üí    }
176‚Üí
177‚Üí    /** Stops the current automatic display if the user has flipped the card */
178‚Üí    fun onShowAnswer() {
179‚Üí        Timber.v("onShowAnswer")
180‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
181‚Üí    }
182‚Üí
183‚Üí    // endregion
184‚Üí
185‚Üí    /**
186‚Üí     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowAnswer]
187‚Üí     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
188‚Üí     */
189‚Üí    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer(additionalDelay: Long = 0) {
190‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
191‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion) return
192‚Üí        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
193‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = true
194‚Üí        delayedShowAnswer(settings.millisecondsToShowQuestionFor + additionalDelay)
195‚Üí    }
196‚Üí
197‚Üí    /**
198‚Üí     * If enabled in preferences, call [AutomaticallyAnswered.automaticShowQuestion]
199‚Üí     * after a user-specified duration, plus an additional delay for media
200‚Üí     */
201‚Üí    fun scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion(additionalMediaDelay: Long = 0) {
202‚Üí        if (!settings.useTimer) return
203‚Üí        if (!settings.autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer) return
204‚Üí        if (hasPlayedSounds) return
205‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = true
206‚Üí        delayedShowQuestion(settings.millisecondsToShowAnswerFor + additionalMediaDelay)
207‚Üí    }
208‚Üí
209‚Üí    fun isEnabled(): Boolean {
210‚Üí        return settings.useTimer
211‚Üí    }
212‚Üí
213‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
214‚Üí    internal fun simulateCardFlip() {
215‚Üí        Timber.d("simulateCardFlip")
216‚Üí        hasPlayedSounds = false
217‚Üí    }
218‚Üí
219‚Üí    interface AutomaticallyAnswered {
220‚Üí        fun automaticShowAnswer()
221‚Üí        fun automaticShowQuestion(action: AutomaticAnswerAction)
222‚Üí    }
223‚Üí
224‚Üí    companion object {
225‚Üí        @CheckResult
226‚Üí        fun defaultInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered): AutomaticAnswer {
227‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswer(target, AutomaticAnswerSettings())
228‚Üí        }
229‚Üí
230‚Üí        @CheckResult
231‚Üí        fun createInstance(target: AutomaticallyAnswered, preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswer {
232‚Üí            val settings = AutomaticAnswerSettings.createInstance(preferences, col)
233‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswer(target, settings)
234‚Üí        }
235‚Üí    }
236‚Üí}
237‚Üí
238‚Üí/**
239‚Üí * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
240‚Üí *
241‚Üí * * Enabled
242‚Üí * * Time to show answer (20s)
243‚Üí * * Time to show next question (60s)
244‚Üí * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
245‚Üí *
246‚Üí * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
247‚Üí *
248‚Üí * These settings also exist in an options group
249‚Üí * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
250‚Üí *
251‚Üí * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
252‚Üí * * Enabled, etc...
253‚Üí */
254‚Üíclass AutomaticAnswerSettings(
255‚Üí    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
256‚Üí    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
257‚Üí    private val secondsToShowQuestionFor: Double = 60.0,
258‚Üí    private val secondsToShowAnswerFor: Double = 20.0
259‚Üí) {
260‚Üí
261‚Üí    val millisecondsToShowQuestionFor = (secondsToShowQuestionFor * 1000L).toLong()
262‚Üí    val millisecondsToShowAnswerFor = (secondsToShowAnswerFor * 1000L).toLong()
263‚Üí
264‚Üí    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
265‚Üí    val autoAdvanceIfShowingAnswer; get() = secondsToShowAnswerFor > 0
266‚Üí    val autoAdvanceIfShowingQuestion; get() = secondsToShowQuestionFor > 0
267‚Üí
268‚Üí    companion object {
269‚Üí        /**
270‚Üí         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config
271‚Üí         */
272‚Üí        @NeedsTest("ensure question setting maps to question parameter")
273‚Üí        fun queryOptions(
274‚Üí            preferences: SharedPreferences,
275‚Üí            col: Collection,
276‚Üí            selectedDid: DeckId
277‚Üí        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
278‚Üí            val conf = col.decks.configDictForDeckId(selectedDid)
279‚Üí            val action = getAction(conf)
280‚Üí            val useTimer = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
281‚Üí
282‚Üí            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(
283‚Üí                answerAction = action,
284‚Üí                useTimer = useTimer,
285‚Üí                secondsToShowQuestionFor = conf.secondsToShowQuestion,
286‚Üí                secondsToShowAnswerFor = conf.secondsToShowAnswer
287‚Üí            )
288‚Üí        }
289‚Üí
290‚Üí        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
291‚Üí            return queryOptions(preferences, col, col.decks.selected())
292‚Üí        }
293‚Üí
294‚Üí        private fun getAction(conf: DeckConfig): AutomaticAnswerAction {
295‚Üí            return try {
296‚Üí                val value: Int = conf.optInt(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY)
297‚Üí                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromConfigValue(value)
298‚Üí            } catch (e: Exception) {
299‚Üí                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
300‚Üí            }
301‚Üí        }
302‚Üí    }
303‚Üí}
304‚Üí
305‚Üí/**
306‚Üí * Represents a value from [anki.deck_config.DeckConfig.Config.AnswerAction]
307‚Üí * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
308‚Üí */
309‚Üíenum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val configValue: Int) {
310‚Üí    /** Default: least invasive action */
311‚Üí    BURY_CARD(0),
312‚Üí    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
313‚Üí    ANSWER_GOOD(2),
314‚Üí    ANSWER_HARD(3),
315‚Üí    SHOW_REMINDER(4);
316‚Üí
317‚Üí    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
318‚Üí        val action = this.toCommand()
319‚Üí        if (action != null) {
320‚Üí            Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
321‚Üí            reviewer.executeCommand(action)
322‚Üí        } else {
323‚Üí            reviewer.showSnackbar(TR.studyingAnswerTimeElapsed())
324‚Üí        }
325‚Üí    }
326‚Üí
327‚Üí    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
328‚Üí    private fun toCommand(): ViewerCommand? {
329‚Üí        return when (this) {
330‚Üí            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
331‚Üí            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand()
332‚Üí            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand()
333‚Üí            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand()
334‚Üí            else -> null
335‚Üí        }
336‚Üí    }
337‚Üí
338‚Üí    companion object {
339‚Üí        /**
340‚Üí         * An integer representing the action when Automatic Answer flips a card from answer to question
341‚Üí         *
342‚Üí         * @see AutomaticAnswerAction
343‚Üí         */
344‚Üí        const val CONFIG_KEY = "answerAction"
345‚Üí
346‚Üí        /** convert from [anki.deck_config.DeckConfig.Config.AnswerAction] to the enum */
347‚Üí        fun fromConfigValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
348‚Üí            return entries.firstOrNull { it.configValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD
349‚Üí        }
350‚Üí    }
351‚Üí}

 36‚Üí/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 37‚Üíenum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 38‚Üí    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),

 80‚Üí    USER_ACTION_2(R.string.user_action_2),
 81‚Üí    USER_ACTION_3(R.string.user_action_3),
 82‚Üí    USER_ACTION_4(R.string.user_action_4),

 84‚Üí    USER_ACTION_6(R.string.user_action_6),

 90‚Üí    companion object {
 91‚Üí        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 92‚Üí            get() = Arrays.stream(entries.toTypedArray())
 93‚Üí                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 94‚Üí                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí        fun fromPreferenceKey(key: String) = entries.first { it.preferenceKey == key }
 97‚Üí    }
 98‚Üí
 99‚Üí    val preferenceKey: String
100‚Üí        get() = "binding_$name"
101‚Üí
102‚Üí    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
103‚Üí        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
104‚Üí            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
105‚Üí            collection.remove(element)
106‚Üí            collection.add(0, element)
107‚Üí            true
108‚Üí        }
109‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
110‚Üí    }
111‚Üí
112‚Üí    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
113‚Üí        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
114‚Üí            // do not reorder the elements
115‚Üí            if (collection.contains(element)) {
116‚Üí                return@BiFunction false
117‚Üí            }
118‚Üí            collection.add(element)
119‚Üí            return@BiFunction true
120‚Üí        }
121‚Üí        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
122‚Üí    }
123‚Üí
124‚Üí    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
125‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
126‚Üí        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
127‚Üí        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
128‚Üí        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
129‚Üí    }
130‚Üí
131‚Üí    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
132‚Üí        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
133‚Üí        bindings.remove(binding)
134‚Üí        prefs.edit {
135‚Üí            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
136‚Üí        }
137‚Üí    }
138‚Üí
139‚Üí    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
140‚Üí    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
141‚Üí        get() {
142‚Üí            // all of the default commands are currently for the Reviewer
143‚Üí            fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, modifierKeys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()) =
144‚Üí                keyCode(keycode, Screen.Reviewer(side), modifierKeys)
145‚Üí            fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide) = unicode(c, Screen.Reviewer(side))
146‚Üí            return when (this) {
147‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> listOf(
148‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
149‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
150‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
151‚Üí                )
152‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> listOf(
153‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
154‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
155‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
156‚Üí                )
157‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> listOf(
158‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
159‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
160‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
161‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
162‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
163‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
164‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
165‚Üí                )
166‚Üí                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> listOf(
167‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
168‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
169‚Üí                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
170‚Üí                )
171‚Üí                EDIT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
172‚Üí                MARK -> listOf(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
173‚Üí                BURY_CARD -> listOf(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
174‚Üí                BURY_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
175‚Üí                SUSPEND_CARD -> listOf(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
176‚Üí                SUSPEND_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
177‚Üí                PLAY_MEDIA -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
178‚Üí                REPLAY_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
179‚Üí                RECORD_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
180‚Üí                SAVE_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_S, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
181‚Üí                UNDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
182‚Üí                REDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH, ModifierKeys(shift = true, ctrl = true, alt = false)))
183‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
184‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
185‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
186‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
187‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
188‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
189‚Üí                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
190‚Üí                SHOW_HINT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
191‚Üí                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
192‚Üí                ADD_NOTE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
193‚Üí                else -> emptyList()
194‚Üí            }
195‚Üí        }
196‚Üí
197‚Üí    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, screen: Screen, keys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()): MappableBinding {
198‚Üí        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), screen)
199‚Üí    }
200‚Üí
201‚Üí    private fun unicode(c: Char, screen: Screen): MappableBinding {
202‚Üí        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), screen)
203‚Üí    }
204‚Üí
205‚Üí    fun interface CommandProcessor {
206‚Üí        /**
207‚Üí         * @return whether the command was executed
208‚Üí         */
209‚Üí        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
210‚Üí    }
211‚Üí}

348‚Üí    private val editCurrentCardLauncher = registerForActivityResult(
349‚Üí        ActivityResultContracts.StartActivityForResult(),
350‚Üí        FlashCardViewerResultCallback { result, reloadRequired ->
351‚Üí            if (result.resultCode == RESULT_OK) {
352‚Üí                Timber.i("AbstractFlashcardViewer:: card edited...")
353‚Üí                onEditedNoteChanged()
354‚Üí            } else if (result.resultCode == RESULT_CANCELED && !reloadRequired) {
355‚Üí                // nothing was changed by the note editor so just redraw the card
356‚Üí                redrawCard()
357‚Üí            }
358‚Üí        }
359‚Üí    )
360‚Üí
361‚Üí    protected inner class FlashCardViewerResultCallback(
362‚Üí        private val callback: (result: ActivityResult, reloadRequired: Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> }
363‚Üí    ) : ActivityResultCallback<ActivityResult> {
364‚Üí        override fun onActivityResult(result: ActivityResult) {
365‚Üí            if (result.resultCode == DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR) {
366‚Üí                closeReviewer(DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR)
367‚Üí            }
368‚Üí            if (result.resultCode == DeckPicker.RESULT_MEDIA_EJECTED) {
369‚Üí                finishNoStorageAvailable()
370‚Üí            }
371‚Üí
372‚Üí            /* Reset the schedule and reload the latest card off the top of the stack if required.
373‚Üí               The card could have been rescheduled, the deck could have changed, or a change of
374‚Üí               note type could have lead to the card being deleted */
375‚Üí            val reloadRequired =
376‚Üí                result.data?.getBooleanExtra(NoteEditor.RELOAD_REQUIRED_EXTRA_KEY, false) == true
377‚Üí            if (reloadRequired) {
378‚Üí                performReload()
379‚Üí            }
380‚Üí
381‚Üí            callback(result, reloadRequired)
382‚Üí        }
383‚Üí    }

387‚Üí    }

389‚Üí    // Event handler for eases (answer buttons)
390‚Üí    inner class SelectEaseHandler : View.OnClickListener, OnTouchListener {
391‚Üí        private var prevCard: Card? = null
392‚Üí        private var hasBeenTouched = false
393‚Üí        private var touchX = 0f
394‚Üí        private var touchY = 0f
395‚Üí        override fun onTouch(view: View, event: MotionEvent): Boolean {
396‚Üí            if (event.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_DOWN) {
397‚Üí                // Save states when button pressed
398‚Üí                prevCard = currentCard
399‚Üí                hasBeenTouched = true
400‚Üí                // We will need to check if a touch is followed by a click
401‚Üí                // Since onTouch always come before onClick, we should check if
402‚Üí                // the touch is going to be a click by storing the start coordinates
403‚Üí                // and comparing with the end coordinates of the touch
404‚Üí                touchX = event.rawX
405‚Üí                touchY = event.rawY
406‚Üí            } else if (event.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
407‚Üí                val diffX = abs(event.rawX - touchX)
408‚Üí                val diffY = abs(event.rawY - touchY)
409‚Üí                // If a click is not coming then we reset the touch
410‚Üí                if (diffX > Companion.CLICK_ACTION_THRESHOLD || diffY > Companion.CLICK_ACTION_THRESHOLD) {
411‚Üí                    hasBeenTouched = false
412‚Üí                }
413‚Üí            }
414‚Üí            return false
415‚Üí        }
416‚Üí
417‚Üí        override fun onClick(view: View) {
418‚Üí            // Try to perform intended action only if the button has been pressed for current card,
419‚Üí            // or if the button was not touched,
420‚Üí            if (prevCard === currentCard || !hasBeenTouched) {

320‚Üí            internal fun upgradeBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey: String, binding: Binding) {
321‚Üí                Timber.d("Replacing gesture '%s' with binding", oldGesturePreferenceKey)
322‚Üí
323‚Üí                // This exists as a user may have mapped "volume down" to "UNDO".
324‚Üí                // Undo already exists as a key binding, and we don't want to trash this during an upgrade
325‚Üí                if (!preferences.contains(oldGesturePreferenceKey)) {
326‚Üí                    Timber.v("No preference to upgrade")
327‚Üí                    return
328‚Üí                }
329‚Üí
330‚Üí                try {
331‚Üí                    replaceBinding(preferences, oldGesturePreferenceKey, binding)
332‚Üí                } finally {
333‚Üí                    Timber.v("removing pref key: '%s'", oldGesturePreferenceKey)
334‚Üí                    // remove the old key
335‚Üí                    preferences.edit { remove(oldGesturePreferenceKey) }
336‚Üí                }

19‚Üíimport com.ichi2.anki.asyncIO

25‚Üí
26‚Üí/**
27‚Üí * Implementation of the `Auto Advance` deck options
28‚Üí *
29‚Üí * A timer (in seconds) can be set to automatically trigger an action after it runs out,
30‚Üí * either in the question side ([QuestionAction]) or in the answer side ([AnswerAction]).
31‚Üí *
32‚Üí * If a timer is set to 0, the corresponding action is not triggered.
33‚Üí *
34‚Üí * @see AutoAdvanceSettings
35‚Üí */
36‚Üíclass AutoAdvance(val viewModel: ReviewerViewModel) {
37‚Üí    private var questionActionJob: Job? = null
38‚Üí    private var answerActionJob: Job? = null
39‚Üí
40‚Üí    private var settings = viewModel.asyncIO {
41‚Üí        val card = viewModel.currentCard.await()
42‚Üí        AutoAdvanceSettings.createInstance(card.currentDeckId().did)
43‚Üí    }
44‚Üí
45‚Üí    private suspend fun durationToShowQuestionFor() = settings.await().durationToShowQuestionFor
46‚Üí    private suspend fun durationToShowAnswerFor() = settings.await().durationToShowAnswerFor
47‚Üí    private suspend fun questionAction() = settings.await().questionAction
48‚Üí    private suspend fun answerAction() = settings.await().answerAction
49‚Üí    suspend fun shouldWaitForAudio() = settings.await().waitForAudio
50‚Üí
51‚Üí    fun cancelQuestionAndAnswerActionJobs() {
52‚Üí        questionActionJob?.cancel()
53‚Üí        answerActionJob?.cancel()
54‚Üí    }
55‚Üí
56‚Üí    fun onCardChange(card: Card) {
57‚Üí        cancelQuestionAndAnswerActionJobs()
58‚Üí        settings = viewModel.asyncIO {
59‚Üí            AutoAdvanceSettings.createInstance(card.currentDeckId().did)
60‚Üí        }
61‚Üí    }
62‚Üí
63‚Üí    suspend fun onShowQuestion() {
64‚Üí        answerActionJob?.cancel()
65‚Üí        if (!durationToShowQuestionFor().isPositive()) return
66‚Üí
67‚Üí        questionActionJob = viewModel.launchCatchingIO {
68‚Üí            delay(durationToShowQuestionFor())
69‚Üí            when (questionAction()) {
70‚Üí                QuestionAction.SHOW_ANSWER -> viewModel.showAnswer()
71‚Üí                QuestionAction.SHOW_REMINDER -> showReminder(TR.studyingQuestionTimeElapsed())
72‚Üí            }
73‚Üí        }
74‚Üí    }
75‚Üí
76‚Üí    suspend fun onShowAnswer() {
77‚Üí        questionActionJob?.cancel()
78‚Üí        if (!durationToShowAnswerFor().isPositive()) return
79‚Üí
80‚Üí        answerActionJob = viewModel.launchCatchingIO {
81‚Üí            delay(durationToShowAnswerFor())
82‚Üí            when (answerAction()) {
83‚Üí                AnswerAction.BURY_CARD -> viewModel.buryCard()
84‚Üí                AnswerAction.ANSWER_AGAIN -> viewModel.answerAgain()
85‚Üí                AnswerAction.ANSWER_HARD -> viewModel.answerHard()
86‚Üí                AnswerAction.ANSWER_GOOD -> viewModel.answerGood()
87‚Üí                AnswerAction.SHOW_REMINDER -> showReminder(TR.studyingAnswerTimeElapsed())
88‚Üí            }
89‚Üí        }
90‚Üí    }
91‚Üí
92‚Üí    private fun showReminder(message: String) {
93‚Üí        viewModel.launchCatchingIO {
94‚Üí            viewModel.actionFeedbackFlow.emit(message)
95‚Üí        }
96‚Üí    }
97‚Üí}

1913‚Üí    /**
1914‚Üí     * Re-renders the content inside the WebView, retaining the side of the card to render
1915‚Üí     *
1916‚Üí     * To be used if card/note data has changed
1917‚Üí     *
1918‚Üí     * @see updateCardAndRedraw - also calls [updateCurrentCard] and resets the side
1919‚Üí     * @see refreshIfRequired - calls through to [updateCurrentCard]
1920‚Üí     */
1921‚Üí    private fun reloadWebViewContent() {
1922‚Üí        currentCard?.renderOutput(getColUnsafe, reload = true, browser = false)
1923‚Üí        if (!isDisplayingAnswer) {
1924‚Üí            Timber.d("displayCardQuestion()")
1925‚Üí            displayAnswer = false

1932‚Üí                answerField?.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1933‚Üí                answerField?.applyLanguageHint(typeAnswer?.languageHint)
1934‚Üí            } else {
1935‚Üí                answerField?.visibility = View.GONE
1936‚Üí            }
1937‚Üí            val content = cardRenderContext!!.renderCard(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, SingleCardSide.FRONT)
1938‚Üí            automaticAnswer.onDisplayQuestion()
1939‚Üí            updateCard(content)
1940‚Üí            hideEaseButtons()
1941‚Üí            Timber.i(
1942‚Üí                "AbstractFlashcardViewer:: Question successfully shown for card id %d",
1943‚Üí                currentCard!!.id
1944‚Üí            )
1945‚Üí        } else {
1946‚Üí            displayCardAnswer()
1947‚Üí        }
1948‚Üí    }

1344‚Üí    open fun displayCardQuestion() {
1345‚Üí        Timber.d("displayCardQuestion()")
1346‚Üí        displayAnswer = false
1347‚Üí        backButtonPressedToReturn = false
1348‚Üí        setInterface()
1349‚Üí        typeAnswer?.input = ""
1350‚Üí        typeAnswer?.updateInfo(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, resources)
1351‚Üí        if (typeAnswer?.validForEditText() == true) {
1352‚Üí            // Show text entry based on if the user wants to write the answer
1353‚Üí            answerField?.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1354‚Üí            answerField?.applyLanguageHint(typeAnswer?.languageHint)
1355‚Üí        } else {
1356‚Üí            answerField?.visibility = View.GONE
1357‚Üí        }
1358‚Üí        val content = cardRenderContext!!.renderCard(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, SingleCardSide.FRONT)
1359‚Üí        automaticAnswer.onDisplayQuestion()
1360‚Üí        launchCatchingTask {
1361‚Üí            if (!automaticAnswerShouldWaitForAudio()) {
1362‚Üí                automaticAnswer.scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer()
1363‚Üí            }
1364‚Üí        }
1365‚Üí        updateCard(content)
1366‚Üí        hideEaseButtons()
1367‚Üí        // If Card-based TTS is enabled, we "automatic display" after the TTS has finished as we don't know the duration
1368‚Üí        Timber.i(
1369‚Üí            "AbstractFlashcardViewer:: Question successfully shown for card id %d",
1370‚Üí            currentCard!!.id
1371‚Üí        )
1372‚Üí    }

1374‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PROTECTED)
1375‚Üí    open fun displayCardAnswer() {
1376‚Üí        // #7294 Required in case the animation end action does not fire:
1377‚Üí        actualHideEaseButtons()
1378‚Üí        Timber.d("displayCardAnswer()")
1379‚Üí        mediaErrorHandler.onCardSideChange()
1380‚Üí        backButtonPressedToReturn = false
1381‚Üí
1382‚Üí        // prevent answering (by e.g. gestures) before card is loaded
1383‚Üí        if (currentCard == null) {
1384‚Üí            return
1385‚Üí        }
1386‚Üí
1387‚Üí        // TODO needs testing: changing a card's model without flipping it back to the front
1388‚Üí        //  (such as editing a card, then editing the card template)
1389‚Üí        typeAnswer!!.updateInfo(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, resources)
1390‚Üí
1391‚Üí        // Explicitly hide the soft keyboard. It *should* be hiding itself automatically,
1392‚Üí        // but sometimes failed to do so (e.g. if an OnKeyListener is attached).
1393‚Üí        if (typeAnswer!!.validForEditText()) {
1394‚Üí            val inputMethodManager = getSystemService(INPUT_METHOD_SERVICE) as InputMethodManager
1395‚Üí            inputMethodManager.hideSoftInputFromWindow(answerField!!.windowToken, 0)
1396‚Üí        }
1397‚Üí        displayAnswer = true
1398‚Üí        answerField!!.visibility = View.GONE
1399‚Üí        // Clean up the user answer and the correct answer
1400‚Üí        if (!typeAnswer!!.useInputTag) {
1401‚Üí            typeAnswer!!.input = answerField!!.text.toString()
1402‚Üí        }
1403‚Üí        isSelecting = false
1404‚Üí        val answerContent = cardRenderContext!!.renderCard(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!, SingleCardSide.BACK)
1405‚Üí        automaticAnswer.onDisplayAnswer()
1406‚Üí        launchCatchingTask {
1407‚Üí            if (!automaticAnswerShouldWaitForAudio()) {
1408‚Üí                automaticAnswer.scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion()
1409‚Üí            }
1410‚Üí        }
1411‚Üí        updateCard(answerContent)
1412‚Üí        displayAnswerBottomBar()
1413‚Üí    }

1440‚Üí        val eUp = MotionEvent.obtain(

 27‚Üíclass ActionButtonStatus {

 70‚Üí    private fun setupButton(preferences: SharedPreferences, @IdRes resourceId: Int, preferenceName: String, showAsActionType: Int) {
 71‚Üí        customButtons[resourceId] = preferences.getString(
 72‚Üí            preferenceName,
 73‚Üí            showAsActionType.toString()
 74‚Üí        )!!.toInt()
 75‚Üí    }
 76‚Üí
 77‚Üí    fun setCustomButtons(menu: Menu) {
 78‚Üí        for ((itemId, value) in customButtons) {
 79‚Üí            if (value != MENU_DISABLED) {
 80‚Üí                val item = menu.findItem(itemId)
 81‚Üí                item.setShowAsAction(value)
 82‚Üí            } else {
 83‚Üí                menu.findItem(itemId).isVisible = false
 84‚Üí            }
 85‚Üí        }
 86‚Üí    }
 87‚Üí
 88‚Üí    fun hideWhiteboardIsDisabled(): Boolean {
 89‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_hide_whiteboard] == MENU_DISABLED
 90‚Üí    }

 94‚Üí    }

102‚Üí    }

106‚Üí    }

109‚Üí        return customButtons[R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color] == MENU_DISABLED
110‚Üí    }

113‚Üí

115‚Üí
