 79‚Üí/**
 80‚Üí * Show a snackbar.
 81‚Üí *
 82‚Üí * You can create snackbars by calling `showSnackbar` on either an activity or a view.
 83‚Üí * As `CoordinatorLayout` is responsible for proper placement and animation of snackbars,
 84‚Üí *
 85‚Üí *   * if calling on an activity, the activity **MUST** have a `CoordinatorLayout`
 86‚Üí *     with id `root_layout`;
 87‚Üí *
 88‚Üí *   * if calling on a view, the view **MUST** be either a `CoordinatorLayout`,
 89‚Üí *     or a (possibly indirect) child of `CoordinatorLayout`.
 90‚Üí *
 91‚Üí * Any additional configuration can be done in the configuration block, e.g.
 92‚Üí *
 93‚Üí *     showSnackbar(text) {
 94‚Üí *         addCallback(callback)
 95‚Üí *     }
 96‚Üí *
 97‚Üí * @receiver An [Activity] that has a [CoordinatorLayout] with id `root_layout`.
 98‚Üí * @param text Text to show, can be formatted.
 99‚Üí * @param duration Optional. For how long to show the snackbar. Can be one of:
100‚Üí *     [Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT], [Snackbar.LENGTH_LONG] (default), [Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE],
101‚Üí *     or exact duration in milliseconds.
102‚Üí * @param snackbarBuilder Optional. A configuration block with the [Snackbar] as `this`.
103‚Üí */
104‚Üífun Activity.showSnackbar(
105‚Üí    text: CharSequence,
106‚Üí    duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_LONG,
107‚Üí    snackbarBuilder: SnackbarBuilder? = null
108‚Üí) {
109‚Üí    val view: View? = findViewById(R.id.root_layout) as? CoordinatorLayout
110‚Üí
111‚Üí    if (view != null) {
112‚Üí        val baseSnackbarBuilder = (this as? BaseSnackbarBuilderProvider)?.baseSnackbarBuilder
113‚Üí        view.showSnackbar(text, duration) {
114‚Üí            baseSnackbarBuilder?.invoke(this)
115‚Üí            snackbarBuilder?.invoke(this)
116‚Üí            Timber.d("displayed snackbar: '%s'", text)
117‚Üí        }
118‚Üí    } else {
119‚Üí        val errorMessage = "While trying to show a snackbar, " +
120‚Üí            "could not find a view with id root_layout in $this"
121‚Üí
122‚Üí        if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
123‚Üí            throw IllegalArgumentException(errorMessage)
124‚Üí        } else {
125‚Üí            Timber.e(errorMessage)
126‚Üí            showThemedToast(this, text, false)
127‚Üí        }
128‚Üí    }
129‚Üí}

 61‚Üí * If the "next question" is shown, "Again" is pressed.
 62‚Üí *
 63‚Üí * The implementation originally had multiple bugs regarding double presses of the answer buttons.
 64‚Üí * This is because the user can press show the card at the same instant that the automatic code executes
 65‚Üí *
 66‚Üí * This has been worked around via the calls: [onSelectEase] and [onShowAnswer].
 67‚Üí * Both of which clear the task queue for the question ([onSelectEase] or vice-versa)
 68‚Üí */
 69‚Üíclass AutomaticAnswer(
 70‚Üí    target: AutomaticallyAnswered,
 71‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting val settings: AutomaticAnswerSettings
 72‚Üí) {
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üí    /** Whether any tasks should be executed/scheduled.
 75‚Üí     *
 76‚Üí     * Ensures that auto answer does not occur if the reviewer is minimised
 77‚Üí     */
 78‚Üí    var isDisabled: Boolean = false
 79‚Üí        private set
 80‚Üí
 81‚Üí    /**
 82‚Üí     * User-controlled pause/resume of auto-advance while reviewing.
 83‚Üí     */
 84‚Üí    var isPausedByUser: Boolean = false
 85‚Üí        private set
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí    private fun isEffectivelyDisabled(): Boolean = isDisabled || isPausedByUser
 88‚Üí
 89‚Üí    /**
 90‚Üí     * Whether the sounds for the current side of the card have been played
 91‚Üí     * So [scheduleAutomaticDisplayAnswer] and [scheduleAutomaticDisplayQuestion] do nothing
 92‚Üí     * if called multiple times on the same side of the card
 93‚Üí     */
 94‚Üí    private var hasPlayedSounds: Boolean = false
 95‚Üí
 96‚Üí    private val showAnswerTask = Runnable {
 97‚Üí        if (isEffectivelyDisabled()) {
 98‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
 99‚Üí            return@Runnable
100‚Üí        }
101‚Üí        target.automaticShowAnswer()
102‚Üí    }
103‚Üí    private val showQuestionTask = Runnable {
104‚Üí        if (isEffectivelyDisabled()) {
105‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
106‚Üí            return@Runnable
107‚Üí        }
108‚Üí        target.automaticShowQuestion(settings.answerAction)
109‚Üí    }
110‚Üí
111‚Üí    /**
112‚Üí     * Handler for the delay in auto showing question and/or answer
113‚Üí     * One toggle for both question and answer, could set longer delay for auto next question
114‚Üí     */
115‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
116‚Üí    val timeoutHandler = HandlerUtils.newHandler()
117‚Üí
118‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
119‚Üí    fun delayedShowQuestion(delay: Long) {
120‚Üí        if (isEffectivelyDisabled()) {
121‚Üí            Timber.d("showQuestion: disabled")
122‚Üí            return
123‚Üí        }
124‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing question in %dms", delay)
125‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showQuestionTask, delay)
126‚Üí    }
127‚Üí
128‚Üí    @VisibleForTesting
129‚Üí    fun delayedShowAnswer(delay: Long) {
130‚Üí        if (isEffectivelyDisabled()) {
131‚Üí            Timber.d("showAnswer: disabled")
132‚Üí            return
133‚Üí        }
134‚Üí        Timber.i("Automatically showing answer in %dms", delay)
135‚Üí        timeoutHandler.postDelayed(showAnswerTask, delay)
136‚Üí    }
137‚Üí
138‚Üí    private fun stopShowQuestionTask() {
139‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show question")
140‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showQuestionTask)
141‚Üí    }
142‚Üí
143‚Üí    private fun stopShowAnswerTask() {
144‚Üí        Timber.i("stop: automatically show answer")
145‚Üí        timeoutHandler.removeCallbacks(showAnswerTask)
146‚Üí    }
147‚Üí
148‚Üí    fun enable() {
149‚Üí        isDisabled = false
150‚Üí    }
151‚Üí
152‚Üí    fun disable() {
153‚Üí        isDisabled = true

155‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
156‚Üí    }
157‚Üí
158‚Üí    fun pauseByUser() {
159‚Üí        isPausedByUser = true
160‚Üí        stopShowAnswerTask()
161‚Üí        stopShowQuestionTask()
162‚Üí    }
163‚Üí
164‚Üí    fun resumeByUser() {

166‚Üí    }
167‚Üí

170‚Üí    }
